name: Earthjustice
mission: Stay informed on how we hold accountable those who break our environmental laws.
theoryOfChange: Earthjustice has a published "Commitment To Justice" page that details a "justice framework" developed by their Community, Justice, and Impact (CJI) team. This framework outlines a clear vision for advancing justice in their interactions, choices, and work, aiming to achieve a truly democratic multi-racial society that incorporates and serves all people and the planet. It emphasizes critical inquiry as a key tool to interrogate choices and understand the organization's impact. The framework guides every staff member to engage in a practice of justice that is informed, builds interconnectedness, encourages inclusive innovation, and maintains values-based accountability.

scoreSummary: Earthjustice shows good impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Annually recognized by the nation’s chief watchdog groups for charitable organizations for transparency and efficiency

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 2.4% of revenue ($2,982,698 on $125,960,142)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.evidence: Niche cause: UNKNOWN (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 27/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.evidence: Board: 33 members, but For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny. — oversight concern
components.name: Governance

judge_issues.message: Evaluation source domain 'charitywatch.org' is not a known trusted evaluator

risks.description: High domestic burn: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
CEO compensation is 2.4% of revenue ($2,982,698 on $125,960,142)

score_details.score_summary: Earthjustice shows good impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://earthjusticehelp.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13119586070029-Can-I-make-a-donation-to-Earthjustice-through-my-Donor-Advised-Fund)

all_citations.claim: Earthjustice operates programs such as The Wild and Healthy Communities and focuses on legal advocacy.
The organization reported total revenue of $125,960,142, a program expense ratio of 80.2%, and 19.3 months of working capital.
Donations to Earthjustice are accepted as general charitable contributions (sadaqah).

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice earns a moderate overall score reflecting its exceptional financial health and efficiency, balanced against the high-cost, indirect nature of systemic legal advocacy. While it is a highly stable organization, its alignment with traditional donor-fit metrics for this specific audience is low.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary due to the nature of long-term legal advocacy and capacity building
Limited direct alignment with specific Muslim community donor priorities

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated at 27/50, reflecting a low donor fit for those seeking traditional community-based projects, though the cause urgency for environmental advocacy remains high [1]. Contributions are considered general charitable giving or sadaqah [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a moderate impact score of 29/50, as its work focuses on capacity building and systemic legal change rather than direct service delivery [1]. While it maintains a strong program expense ratio of 80.2%, the nature of high-level litigation results in a high cost per beneficiary [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Earthjustice serves as a premier nonprofit public interest law organization, leveraging legal expertise and partnerships to protect the environment and public health.
strengths: High operational efficiency with a fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised
Robust financial stability maintaining 19.3 months of working capital
Strong commitment to mission-critical work with a program expense ratio of 80.2%
baseline_narrative.summary: Founded as a legal advocate for the planet, Earthjustice operates major programs including The Wild and Healthy Communities to enforce environmental protections [1]. In the 2023 fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $125,960,142 to support its litigation and advocacy efforts [2].

all_citations.claim: premier nonprofit public interest environmental law organization
all_citations.quote: Earthjustice is the premier nonprofit public interest environmental law organization.
Your gift is tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law.

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice earns a moderate overall score reflecting its exceptional financial health and efficiency, balanced against the high-cost, indirect nature of systemic legal advocacy. While it is a highly stable organization, its alignment with traditional donor-fit metrics for this specific audience is low.

areas_for_improvement.detail: The nature of legal advocacy results in a high <cite id="13">cost per beneficiary of approximately $118,249</cite>, which may deter donors seeking high-volume direct service metrics.
The <cite id="14">CEO compensation of $518,145</cite> and total officer compensation of nearly $3 million warrants close donor scrutiny relative to total revenue.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's high reserves mitigate the risk of financial instability during economic downturns.
risk_factors: <cite id="21">80.2% of expenses stay in the US</cite> despite some international grantmaking
High executive compensation relative to smaller nonprofits
case_against.summary: The primary case against Earthjustice for this specific donor pool is the lack of religious alignment and the high-overhead nature of legal work.

confidence.notes: Financial data is highly reliable; alignment data is based on the absence of religious indicators.

known_gaps: Not fully rated by Charity Navigator
No specific Muslim donor outreach data

alignment.explanation: Alignment is limited as the charity is <cite id="19">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE only</cite> and focuses on advocacy rather than direct humanitarian relief.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="20">clearly stating its position on zakat</cite> or highlighting programs that serve underserved Muslim populations.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Earthjustice due to its <cite id="15">100% independent board members</cite> and long history of <cite id="16">audited financial statements</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To improve credibility, the organization could provide more granular reporting on the specific outcomes of its international grantmaking.

impact.explanation: Impact is achieved through systemic change, with <cite id="17">79.9% of expenses dedicated to programs</cite> and a documented theory of change.
impact.improvement: If reserves remain significantly above policy targets, the organization should <cite id="18">document specific triggers for planned deployment</cite> of these funds.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Documented Theory of Change and Legal Wins

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports partner organizations to amplify environmental litigation and policy impact.

rich_narrative.headline: Earthjustice serves as a premier nonprofit public interest law organization, leveraging legal expertise and partnerships to protect the environment and public health.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize long-term systemic change through the legal system and are comfortable supporting secular environmental causes with sadaqah.
donor_motivations: Want to use the law to protect the environment
Value institutional stability and long-term litigation
Care about climate change and tribal sovereignty
giving_considerations: Impact is measured in legal precedents, not immediate individual relief
High financial reserves ensure the organization can fight long battles
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate humanitarian aid in crisis zones.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a verified theory of change and tracks outcomes such as legal victories and policy changes.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Earthjustice uses litigation to force government and corporate compliance with environmental laws, creating systemic protections for air, water, and wildlife.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: In legal advocacy, evidence of success is found in court rulings that set long-term precedents.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While highly capitalized, the scale of environmental litigation needs continues to grow.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: United States and limited international partners

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="22">exclusive focus on litigation</cite> rather than general public awareness or lobbying.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="8">19.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive buffer for multi-year legal cases.
Earthjustice demonstrates lean development operations, spending only <cite id="9">$0.12 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
With a history <cite id="10">dating back to 1971</cite>, the organization has over five decades of experience in environmental litigation.

strengths_deep_dive: Earthjustice's strength lies in its ability to sustain prolonged legal challenges that many smaller organizations cannot afford. Their <cite id="11">total assets of $294.7 million</cite> and substantial reserves allow them to take on powerful interests in court. Furthermore, their commitment to transparency is evidenced by a <cite id="12">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, ensuring donors have access to comprehensive organizational data.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1971 as a legal advocate for the planet, Earthjustice has evolved into the <cite id="1">premier nonprofit public interest environmental law organization</cite> in the United States. The organization leverages the power of the judicial system to enforce environmental protections, focusing on <cite id="2">climate and energy, healthy communities, and tribal sovereignty</cite>. In the 2023 fiscal year, Earthjustice reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $125,960,142</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="4">$117.4 million in public contributions</cite>. Their operational model is built on long-term litigation, which aims to secure systemic changes in environmental policy and public health. Despite the high costs associated with complex legal battles, the organization maintains <cite id="5">a program expense ratio of 80.2%</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor funds are directed toward its core mission of environmental defense. Beyond litigation, Earthjustice engages in <cite id="6">significant grantmaking activity</cite>, distributing over <cite id="7">$8.1 million to domestic and international partners</cite> to strengthen the broader environmental movement.
